_comment: Corporate events (M&A, spinoffs, restructuring) that affect quarterly data comparability. Used by the sector heatmap to annotate tickers with known data anomalies. Add new events here when a ticker shows unexpected blanks, negative margins, or extreme YoY jumps.

events.details: Dell completed the spin-off of its ~81% equity ownership in VMware. Post-spinoff, Dell's financials were restated on a continuing-operations basis, but Q1-Q3 FY2022 10-Qs include pre-spinoff VMware figures. This makes the derived Q4 FY2022 (Annual minus 9M) produce artificially negative gross margins (-8.2%).

Lumentum acquired NeoPhotonics Corporation for ~$918M. Post-acquisition quarters include NeoPhotonics revenue and costs, making YoY comparisons to pre-acquisition quarters non-comparable. Fiscal calendar was unaffected but entity composition changed.
events.impact_type: Entity change — YoY non-comparable across acquisition

events.details: Broadcom completed the acquisition of VMware for ~$69B. VMware revenue consolidated into AVGO starting Q1 FY2024 (ending Feb 2024). Pre-acquisition quarters show organic Broadcom only; post-acquisition includes VMware. Q3 FY2024 had a -$1.88B net loss from integration charges.
events.impact_type: Revenue step-up + integration charges — YoY non-comparable

events.details: Microsoft completed the acquisition of Activision Blizzard for ~$69B. Gaming segment revenue increased significantly post-close. Minimal impact on overall margins due to Microsoft's scale, but Gaming segment YoY comparisons are non-comparable.
Amazon completed the acquisition of MGM Studios for ~$8.5B. Relatively small impact on Amazon's overall financials given Amazon's scale (~$500B+ revenue).
events.impact_type: Minor — small relative to Amazon's scale

events.details: Intel announced a restructuring plan including ~15,000 job cuts, Dresden fab closure, and $10B annualized cost reduction target by 2025. Resulted in significant impairment and restructuring charges across multiple quarters.
events.impact_type: Impairment charges — net income significantly affected

events.details: Marvell completed the acquisition of Inphi Corporation for ~$10B. Inphi's high-speed interconnect products were consolidated into Marvell's Data Center segment. Pre-acquisition quarters are Marvell-only.
